title: viola × ravida
intro: Viola × ravida, also known as the hybrid viola or woodland viola, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the violaceae family. Native to Europe and North America, this perennial plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Viola × ravida include its potential to reduce stress, promote relaxation, and improve sleep quality. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and are often used to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
